senses.definition: a method of treatment in which thin needles are inserted in specific spots on the body, originated in China

examples.x: She tried acupuncture to relieve her chronic back pain.
Acupuncture has been practiced in China for thousands of years.
The doctor recommended acupuncture as an alternative to medication.
She was skeptical about acupuncture but decided to give it a try.
Acupuncture involves inserting thin needles into specific points on the body.

senses.definition: a part of a hospital or a healthcare facility that provides care for patients who do not require an overnight stay

examples.x: She visited the dental clinic for her routine check-up and cleaning.
The pediatric clinic provides vaccinations and well-child visits for infants and children.
He attends physical therapy sessions at the sports clinic to rehabilitate his knee injury.
The clinic offers mental health counseling services to support patients dealing with stress and anxiety.
They opened a free clinic in the community to provide healthcare services to underserved populations.

senses.definition: a separate area in a hospital for patients with similar conditions

examples.x: The hospital’s pediatric ward is specifically for children who need medical care.
He was transferred to the orthopedic ward for treatment of his broken leg.
The elderly patients were moved to a different ward designed for their specific care needs.
They visited the psychiatric ward to see a friend who was receiving treatment.
The hospital’s emergency ward is equipped to handle urgent and critical cases.

senses.definition: a room in a hospital or clinic equipped to provide emergency care to people in need of immediate medical treatment

examples.x: He rushed to the emergency room after injuring his ankle during a soccer game.
The emergency room staff responded quickly to stabilize the patient who arrived with severe chest pain.
The hospital's emergency room is open 24 hours a day, seven days a week to treat urgent medical conditions.
She called an ambulance when her husband collapsed at home and was taken to the nearest emergency room.

senses.definition: a field of medicine or a department in a hospital that deals with ear, nose, and throat problems or diseases

examples.x: The ENT doctor recommended surgery to correct his persistent ear infections.
The ENT clinic offers services for hearing loss, allergies, and throat disorders.
They scheduled an appointment with the ENT specialist to address persistent snoring and sleep apnea.
He was referred to an ENT for a thorough evaluation of his persistent sore throat.

senses.definition: a shop where medicines are sold

examples.x: She went to the pharmacy to pick up her prescription and consult the pharmacist about dosage.
The pharmacy offers over-the-counter medications for common ailments like headaches and colds.
The local pharmacy provides vaccination services and health consultations.
The pharmacy was well-stocked with both prescription drugs and health supplements.
They visited the pharmacy for advice on managing a chronic condition with medication.

senses.definition: a patient who stays in the hospital while they receive treatment

examples.x: She was admitted as an inpatient to manage her condition more effectively under constant medical supervision.
The doctor decided to admit him as an inpatient due to the severity of his condition.
He needed to stay as an inpatient for a few days to recover from his procedure.
The treatment plan required that she remain an inpatient to receive intravenous medication.
They scheduled regular check-ups for the inpatient to track progress and adjust treatment.

senses.definition: a patient who receives treatment in a hospital but does not remain there afterward

examples.x: The outpatient arrived at the clinic for a follow-up appointment after his surgery.
Outpatients often appreciate the convenience of receiving medical care without staying overnight in the hospital.
Outpatients typically have appointments scheduled in advance to receive specialized medical care, such as chemotherapy or physical therapy.
Outpatients often benefit from shorter wait times compared to those admitted for inpatient care in hospitals.

senses.definition: the well-being of a person's mind

examples.x: They discussed strategies for improving mental health, such as mindfulness and regular exercise.
He attended a workshop on mental health to learn about coping mechanisms and self-care.
Mental health assessments are important for diagnosing and treating conditions like depression and bipolar disorder.
He attended therapy sessions to address his mental health concerns and improve his well-being.

senses.definition: an intense and irrational fear toward a specific thing such as an object, situation, concept, or animal

examples.x: The phobia of dogs she developed after a childhood incident affects her ability to interact with pets.
They discussed various treatments for phobias, including therapy and relaxation techniques.
The therapist worked with him to overcome his phobia of enclosed spaces through gradual exposure.
His fear of flying is more than just nervousness; it’s a phobia that prevents him from traveling by airplane.
She has a phobia of spiders and feels extremely anxious whenever she sees one.

senses.definition: a doctor who is highly trained in a particular area of medicine

examples.x: She consulted a dermatologist, a specialist in skin conditions, for her persistent rash.
The specialist provided detailed advice on managing the rare disease based on their extensive research.
He saw an orthopedic specialist to get a second opinion on his knee injury.
The specialist’s office is located in the city’s medical district.

senses.definition: a dentist who specializes in the correction, etc. of the position of teeth

examples.x: She visited the orthodontist regularly to adjust her braces and track the progress of her teeth alignment.
The orthodontist recommended a treatment plan to straighten his son's teeth and improve his bite.
Orthodontists undergo specialized training to specialize in orthodontic treatment techniques.
The orthodontist's office is equipped with state-of-the-art technology to create customized braces and aligners.

senses.definition: a trained individual who provides emergency medical care to people before taking them to the hospital

examples.x: The paramedic arrived at the accident scene and immediately began assessing the injured.
She decided to pursue a career as a paramedic to help people in critical situations.
Paramedics play a crucial role in responding to medical emergencies and saving lives.
The ambulance crew includes paramedics who are trained to handle a wide range of medical emergencies.

senses.definition: a doctor who specializes in the treatment of children

examples.x: She took her baby to the pediatrician for routine check-ups and vaccinations.
The pediatrician diagnosed the child with a common cold and recommended rest and fluids.
They consulted a pediatrician to address their concerns about their teenager’s growth and development.
He recommended a pediatrician who could provide specialized care for children with chronic conditions.
The new parents were relieved to find a pediatrician who was both knowledgeable and compassionate.

senses.definition: a doctor who performs medical operation

examples.x: The surgeon successfully completed the operation to remove the tumor from the patient’s abdomen.
She consulted a surgeon to discuss the best approach for her upcoming knee surgery.
The surgeon used advanced techniques to repair the fractured bone during the procedure.
The surgeon’s precision and skill were crucial in ensuring a successful outcome for the patient.
The surgeon explained the risks and benefits of the operation to the patient before proceeding.

senses.definition: a doctor who performs medical operations to repair body parts or make them look more attractive

examples.x: She consulted a plastic surgeon for reconstructive surgery after an accident left her with facial injuries.
The plastic surgeon performed a facelift to help improve the patient’s appearance and self-esteem.
He sought advice from a plastic surgeon about options for scar revision following a previous surgery.
The plastic surgeon's role in the procedure included both aesthetic improvements and functional repairs.
She was pleased with the results of the plastic surgeon’s work, which achieved both her aesthetic and functional goals.

senses.definition: a qualified person whose job is treating people's mental issues by talking with them instead of giving them drugs

examples.x: She visited a therapist to work through her anxiety and stress management.
He found that regular sessions with a therapist were beneficial for addressing his long-term emotional challenges.
The therapist helped him develop coping strategies for dealing with his depression.
The therapist used cognitive-behavioral techniques to assist the patient in changing negative thought patterns.
The therapist provided guidance on handling grief after the loss of a loved one.

senses.definition: an operation performed by medical professionals to diagnose, treat, etc. a medical condition or injury

examples.x: The surgeon explained the details of the procedure scheduled for tomorrow to remove the appendix.
She underwent a minimally invasive procedure to repair a torn ligament in her knee.
Patients are often briefed on the risks and benefits of a procedure before giving consent.

senses.definition: the act of keeping someone or something unharmed

examples.x: The sunscreen provides protection from harmful UV rays during sun exposure.
The company implemented safety protocols to ensure the protection of employees in the workplace.
They wore helmets and pads for protection while riding their bikes.
The insurance policy offers protection against financial losses due to unexpected events.

senses.definition: a tissue or organ that is removed from a body and put into another

examples.x: Despite some early complications, the transplant ultimately took, and she is now on the path to recovery.
His body initially rejected the transplant, so the medical team had to adjust his medication.
He waited for several years on the transplant list before receiving a compatible donor organ.

senses.definition: a medical practice that involves cutting open a body part in order to repair, remove, etc. an organ

examples.x: After the surgery, the patient received instructions on how to care for the surgical wound.
The doctor recommended surgery to remove the tumor and improve the patient’s health.
She underwent surgery to repair a torn ligament in her knee.
The surgery was performed successfully, and the patient is expected to make a full recovery.
They scheduled the surgery for next week, following all necessary pre-operative tests.

senses.definition: a measured amount of drug or medicine that is taken at a given time

examples.x: The doctor prescribed a dose of 500 milligrams of the medication to be taken twice daily.
She forgot to take her morning dose, so she took it as soon as she remembered.
The patient was instructed to take a dose every eight hours to maintain effective levels of the drug.
They calculated the dose based on the patient’s weight and medical condition.

senses.definition: a type of medicine that is used to reduce or relieve pain

examples.x: She took a painkiller to help manage her headache after a long day at work.
The doctor prescribed a stronger painkiller for post-surgery discomfort.
He reached for an over-the-counter painkiller to alleviate his muscle pain from the workout.
They discussed the possible side effects of the painkiller with the pharmacist.
He relied on a painkiller to cope with chronic pain from his condition.

senses.definition: the act of treating mental or physical illnesses

examples.x: They discussed different types of therapy available for managing chronic pain.
She found that a combination of medication and therapy was effective in managing her condition.
The therapy involved exercises and treatments designed to rehabilitate the injured muscle.
She began therapy to address her anxiety and improve her emotional well-being.

senses.definition: an image of the inside of a body created using X-rays
a piece of equipment with a container that supplies oxygen to a person, used in places or situations in which it is difficult to breathe normally

examples.x: The firefighter wore a breathing apparatus to enter the smoke-filled building and rescue trapped occupants.
The scuba diver relies on a breathing apparatus to breathe underwater during deep-sea explorations.
In hospitals, patients with respiratory conditions may require a breathing apparatus to support their breathing.
The emergency responder administered oxygen through a portable breathing apparatus to stabilize the injured hiker.
A breathing apparatus is essential for workers in industrial settings where air quality may be compromised.

senses.definition: relating to the observation, examination, and treatment of patients in a medical setting

examples.x: The doctor made a clinical diagnosis based on the patient's symptoms and medical history.
Nurses play a crucial role in providing clinical care and monitoring patients' vital signs.
The hospital implemented new clinical protocols to improve patient outcomes.

senses.definition: to start experiencing symptoms of an illness

examples.x: She came down with a severe case of the flu and had to stay home from work.
After traveling abroad, he came down with a tropical disease and needed medical treatment.
The entire family came down with food poisoning after eating contaminated seafood.
I think I'm coming down with a cold; I have a sore throat and a runny nose.
He came down with a stomach virus and experienced nausea and vomiting.

senses.definition: to cause a person or thing to return to a state of physical or emotional health

examples.x: The medicine healed his sore throat, allowing him to speak without pain.
The doctor’s treatment helped heal the wound quickly.
The warm sunlight seemed to heal his tired muscles after a long hike.
She used herbal remedies to heal her cold more naturally.

senses.definition: to get in bed for sleeping

examples.x: After a long day of gardening, she was ready to hit the hay and get a good night's rest.
The early morning flight meant I had to hit the hay early the night before.
When the camping trip ended, we couldn't wait to hit the hay in our comfortable sleeping bags.
The children were so tired after playing outside that they were eager to hit the hay as soon as the sun set.

senses.definition: to insert a living tissue or an artificial object into the body via medical procedure

examples.x: The surgeon successfully implanted a pacemaker to regulate the patient's heartbeat.
Dental implants are commonly used to replace missing teeth by surgically implanting artificial tooth roots into the jawbone.
The doctor recommended implanting a stent to improve blood flow through the narrowed artery.
Researchers are exploring the possibility of implanting electronic devices in the brain to assist with certain neurological conditions.
To treat severe arthritis, the orthopedic surgeon suggested implanting an artificial joint in the patient's knee.

senses.definition: to insert a substance or material into the body, often through a needle

examples.x: Veterinarians may inject animals with medications for various health purposes.
The veterinarian injected the dog with a vaccine to prevent disease.
They had to inject the horse with fluids to help with dehydration.
The medic injected the soldier with a sedative to calm him down.
In the emergency room, they injected the patient with fluids to stabilize his condition.

senses.definition: to lose consciousness

examples.x: It was so hot in the room that she felt like she was going to pass out.
He passed out from exhaustion after the marathon.
After standing for hours in the sun, he passed out from dehydration.
She felt dizzy and nearly passed out during the presentation.
Without enough ventilation in the room, several people started to feel like they might pass out.
During the boxing match, a strong punch made him pass out momentarily.
She hit her head against the shelf and passed out instantly.

senses.definition: to decrease the amount of pain, stress, etc.

examples.x: Taking a short break can relieve stress during a busy workday.
The ongoing therapy sessions are currently relieving her emotional distress.
Medication has relieved the intensity of his chronic pain.
A warm bath relieved the muscle tension after a long day.
A good night's sleep will relieve fatigue and improve overall well-being.

senses.definition: (of a medical device) to take a picture of a body part often using X-rays for detailed examinations by a specialist

examples.x: The technician scanned the woman's abdomen to monitor the growth of the fetus.
The radiologist scanned the patient's spine to look for signs of injury or degeneration.
The ultrasound technician scanned the pregnant woman's belly to monitor the health of the fetus.
The specialist scanned the patient's entire body to look for any signs of cancerous growths.
The doctor scanned the patient's chest to check for any abnormalities in the lungs.

senses.definition: to bathe under a continuous flow of water, typically for cleansing the body

examples.x: She showers every morning before going to work.
After a long day of hiking, they were eager to shower and relax.
He prefers to shower in the evening to unwind before bed.
Please remember to shower before entering the swimming pool.
The athletes showered quickly after the game to freshen up.
